text: Ultra-endurance running may lead to right ventricular dysfunction and potential increased risk of arrhythmias and hypertension as one of the clearest long-term effects on the cardiovascular system.
Acute renal injury in ultra-endurance running could lead to progressive renal scarring and chronic kidney disease in rare cases.
Female ultra-endurance athletes may be at greater risk of certain health issues due to interactions between energy availability and sex-hormone concentrations.
Failure to consider sex differences in the design of female-specific ultra-endurance training programs may negatively impact athlete longevity.
Youth, masters, and female athletes may be more susceptible to certain long-term health issues from ultra-endurance running compared to the general ultra-endurance population.
population: youth, masters, and female ultra-endurance runners

text: Serious acute adverse events in ultra-endurance running are rare, but mounting evidence suggests potential long-term maladaptation in cardiovascular, respiratory, musculoskeletal, renal, immunological, gastrointestinal, neurological, and integumentary systems.
